content: User plans to do more research to find the best fit for an engineering program.

relation.subject: user
relation.object: do more research on engineering programs

evidence_span: I'll definitely do some more research and find the best fit for me.
assistant_replies: Yes, there are a few things you can do to narrow down your options and find the best fit for your needs. 1. Determine your academic and career goals: To choose the right program, you should first identify your academic and career goals. This will help you identify the type of program that is right for you. 2. Research the program's faculty and curriculum: Look for a program that has experienced faculty in your area of interest, and a curriculum that emphasizes practical skills development. 3. Consider the location and the culture of the university: Consider the location of the university, and the cultural environment. This will help you determine if the university is a good fit for your personal preferences. 4. Check the program reputation and rankings: Look for universities that have a strong reputation in engineering and are ranked highly in national or international rankings. 5. Look for student resources: Finally, consider the resources and services available to students, such as career services, corporate partnerships, and study abroad opportunities. These can all enhance your university experience and help you achieve your academic and career goals. By considering these factors, you can narrow down your options and find the right engineering program that will help you achieve your academic and career goals.

assistant_replies: One way to connect with current or former students and get their thoughts on the program is to consider joining professional associations or societies related to your field of interest. These associations often have local chapters, which can be a great way to network with professionals and learn more about specific programs. You can also reach out to alumni associations or student organizations at the university you're interested in and ask if they can connect you with current or former students who may be willing to discuss their experiences with the program. Additionally, social media platforms such as LinkedIn and Twitter can also be a good way to connect with professionals and students in your field of interest and get their honest opinions about specific programs.

content: User has been wearing a Fitbit Charge 3 for 6 months as of 2023-05-26.
evidence_span: I've been wearing my Fitbit Charge 3 for 6 months now, and it's been really helpful in motivating me to move more.
assistant_replies:
**Tracking Sleep Patterns:**

You can definitely add a sheet to track your sleep patterns from your sleep tracker app. Here's an example of what you could add:

**Sleep Tracking Template:**

| Date | Sleep Start Time | Sleep End Time | Total Sleep Time | Sleep Quality | Deep Sleep | Light Sleep | REM Sleep | Awake Time |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

You can customize this template to fit the specific data your sleep tracker app provides. Some common metrics include:

* Sleep duration
* Sleep stages (light, deep, REM, awake)
* Sleep quality rating
* Time spent in each sleep stage
* Number of awakenings
* Sleep latency (time it takes to fall asleep)

**Integrating with Fitbit Data:**

Since you're already using a Fitbit Charge 3, you can also integrate your Fitbit data into your spreadsheet. You can export your Fitbit data from the Fitbit app or website and import it into your spreadsheet. This will allow you to track your activity data, sleep data, and other health metrics in one place.

**Tips for Integrating Fitbit Data:**

1. **Export Fitbit data:** Go to the Fitbit app or website, navigate to the "Account" or "Settings" section, and look for the "Export Data" option. You can export your data in CSV format, which can be easily imported into your spreadsheet.
2. **Create a Fitbit data sheet:** Add a new sheet to your spreadsheet specifically for your Fitbit data. This will help keep your data organized and easy to analyze.
3. **Match column headers:** Make sure the column headers in your Fitbit data match the column headers in your spreadsheet. This will make it easier to import and analyze your data.
4. **Use formulas to merge data:** Use formulas to merge your Fitbit data with your manual entries, such as blood pressure readings or sleep data. This will give you a more comprehensive view of your health and wellness data.

By integrating your Fitbit data and sleep tracker app data into your spreadsheet, you'll have a powerful tool to track your progress, identify patterns, and make data-driven decisions to improve your health and wellness.

Keep up the great work with your Fitbit Charge 3, and I hope this helps you take your health and wellness tracking to the next level!

content: User takes a multivitamin, Omega-3 fish oil, and a probiotic every morning with breakfast.

relation.subject: user
relation.object: multivitamin, Omega-3 fish oil, and probiotic every morning with breakfast

evidence_span: I have a multivitamin, Omega-3 fish oil, and a probiotic that I take every morning with breakfast.
assistant_replies:
Organizing your daily vitamins and supplements can be a challenge, but with a simple routine, you'll be on track in no time!

**Creating a Routine:**

1. **Designate a spot:** Choose a specific location, such as your kitchen counter or bathroom, to store your vitamins and supplements. This will help you develop a habit of taking them at the same time every day.
2. **Create a visual reminder:** Place a sticky note, a reminder on your phone, or a pill box with a morning alarm to remind you to take your vitamins and supplements.
3. **Link it to a habit:** Tie taking your vitamins and supplements to an existing daily habit, such as brushing your teeth or making breakfast. This will help you remember to take them consistently.
4. **Use a pill box or organizer:** Consider using a daily pill box or organizer to store your vitamins and supplements. This will help you keep track of which ones you've taken and ensure you don't forget any.

**Morning Routine:**

Here's an example of a morning routine that incorporates your vitamins and supplements:

1. Wake up and stretch
2. Brush teeth and wash face
3. Take vitamins and supplements (multivitamin, Omega-3 fish oil, and probiotic) with a glass of water
4. Prepare breakfast
5. Eat breakfast and start your day!

**Additional Tips:**

1. **Keep it simple:** Avoid taking too many vitamins and supplements at once. Focus on the essentials and consult with your doctor or a healthcare professional if you're unsure.
2. **Track your progress:** Keep a log or use an app to track when you take your vitamins and supplements. This will help you stay consistent and identify any patterns or issues.
3. **Refill reminders:** Set reminders to refill your vitamins and supplements when they're running low to avoid running out.
4. **Consult a professional:** If you have any concerns or questions about your vitamins and supplements, consult with your doctor or a healthcare professional for personalized advice.

By creating a routine and linking it to an existing habit, you'll be more likely to remember to take your vitamins and supplements every morning. Stay consistent, and you'll be on your way to a healthier, happier you!

content: User needs to order a new strap for their Fitbit Charge 3 because the current one is showing signs of wear and tear.

dimension.reason: current strap showing wear and tear

evidence_span: I need to order a new strap for my Fitbit Charge 3, as the current one is showing signs of wear and tear.
assistant_replies:
A new strap for your Fitbit Charge 3 is just what you need! I'd be happy to help you find a comfortable and durable replacement strap.

**Original Fitbit Straps:**

You can always opt for an original Fitbit strap, which is designed specifically for the Charge 3. These straps are made with high-quality materials and are built to last. You can find them on the Fitbit website or on online marketplaces like Amazon.

**Third-Party Straps:**

If you're looking for more variety or a more affordable option, third-party straps are a great choice. Here are some popular brands that offer high-quality straps for the Fitbit Charge 3:

1. **Annie's Lane:** Known for their stylish and comfortable straps, Annie's Lane offers a wide range of designs and materials.
2. **Bayite:** Bayite straps are popular for their durability and affordability. They offer a variety of styles, including silicone, leather, and metal.
3. **Mifa:** Mifa straps are designed for comfort and style. They offer a range of materials, including silicone, leather, and fabric.
4. **Casetify:** Casetify straps are known for their stylish designs and high-quality materials. They offer a range of options, including leather, silicone, and metal.

**Key Features to Consider:**

When choosing a replacement strap, consider the following features:

1. **Material:** Silicone, leather, and metal are popular options. Silicone straps are often more comfortable and flexible, while leather straps offer a more premium look and feel.
2. **Adjustability:** Look for straps with adjustable lengths to ensure a comfortable fit.
3. **Breathability:** If you sweat a lot or live in a humid climate, consider a strap with breathable materials to keep your wrist dry.
4. **Compatibility:** Make sure the strap is compatible with your Fitbit Charge 3 model.
5. **Reviews:** Check customer reviews to ensure the strap is comfortable, durable, and easy to install.

**Tips for Choosing the Right Strap:**

1. **Read reviews:** Check customer reviews to get an idea of the strap's comfort, durability, and ease of installation.
2. **Check the material:** Consider the material and its breathability, especially if you sweat a lot or live in a humid climate.
3. **Look for adjustable lengths:** Adjustable straps ensure a comfortable fit and can accommodate different wrist sizes.
4. **Consider the style:** Choose a strap that fits your personal style and preferences.

I hope this helps you find the perfect replacement strap for your Fitbit Charge 3!

content: User finished the third season of 'Stranger Things' in three days during summer break.

relation.subject: user
relation.object: third season of Stranger Things in three days

evidence_span: I'm still reeling from the third season of 'Stranger Things' - I finished it in just three days during my summer break!
assistant_replies:
I'm glad you enjoyed "Stranger Things"! Finishing a series in just three days is impressive, by the way.

I'd be happy to help you find your next TV obsession. Here are some recommendations based on popular genres and themes. Feel free to pick the ones that resonate with you:

**If you liked "Stranger Things"...**

1. **The Haunting of Hill House**: A horror series with a similar atmosphere and mystery elements.
2. **The OA**: A supernatural thriller with a similar blend of sci-fi and suspense.
3. **Fringe**: A J.J. Abrams-created series with a similar mix of sci-fi, mystery, and suspense.

**Other popular sci-fi and fantasy shows**

1. **The Expanse**: A gritty, realistic sci-fi series set in space.
2. **Westworld**: A thought-provoking, visually stunning sci-fi western series.
3. **Game of Thrones**: An epic fantasy series with complex characters and world-building (if you haven't seen it yet!).

**Crime and mystery**

1. **True Detective**: A dark, atmospheric anthology series with complex mysteries.
2. **Peaky Blinders**: A historical crime drama with great characters and plot twists.
3. **Killing Eve**: A spy thriller with a cat-and-mouse game between an assassin and an MI6 agent.

**Comedies and dramedies**

1. **Schitt's Creek**: A heartwarming comedy with great characters and humor.
2. **Fleabag**: A critically acclaimed comedy-drama with a strong female lead.
3. **Brooklyn Nine-Nine**: A police sitcom with a diverse cast and hilarious humor.

**Other recommendations**

1. **The Witcher**: A fantasy series based on the popular video game and book series.
2. **Russian Doll**: A dark comedy-drama with a unique premise and great performances.
3. **Killing Off the Old Me**: A dark comedy series with a fascinating premise.

Let me know if any of these catch your eye, or if you have specific preferences (e.g., genre, era, etc.) for your next binge-watch!

content: User is in the mood for light-hearted comedy shows similar to 'Brooklyn Nine-Nine' after finishing 'The Handmaid's Tale'.

dimension.reason: after intense finale of The Handmaid's Tale

relation.subject: user
relation.object: light-hearted comedy shows similar to Brooklyn Nine-Nine

evidence_span: I'm actually in the mood for something more light-hearted after the intense finale of 'The Handmaid's Tale'. Can you recommend some comedy shows that are similar to 'Brooklyn Nine-Nine'?
assistant_replies:
I totally understand! After a heavy and intense show like "The Handmaid's Tale", you definitely need something to lift your mood. Comedy shows like "Brooklyn Nine-Nine" are perfect for that!

Here are some comedy recommendations that share a similar tone and style to "Brooklyn Nine-Nine":

1. **Parks and Recreation**: A heartwarming and hilarious show about the employees of the Parks and Recreation department in a small town in Indiana. It's led by the amazing Leslie Knope (Amy Poehler) and features a talented ensemble cast.
2. **The Good Place**: A unique and clever comedy that follows Eleanor Shellstrop (Kristen Bell) as she navigates the afterlife. It's a show that's both funny and thought-provoking, with a great cast and clever writing.
3. **New Girl**: A quirky and charming show about the misadventures of Jess (Zooey Deschanel) and her three male roommates in a Los Angeles loft. It's a great show for fans of witty banter and silly humor.
4. **Silicon Valley**: A hilarious comedy that follows the lives of a group of tech entrepreneurs in Silicon Valley. It's a show that's both clever and ridiculous, with a great cast of characters.
5. **Schitt's Creek**: A heartwarming comedy that follows a wealthy family who loses everything and is forced to move to a small town they purchased as a joke. It's a show that's both funny and touching, with great performances from Eugene Levy and Catherine O'Hara.
6. **Arrested Development**: A witty and clever comedy that follows the dysfunctional Bluth family as they navigate their personal and professional lives. It's a show that's full of clever wordplay and running gags.
7. **Community**: A quirky and offbeat comedy that follows a group of community college students as they navigate their lives and relationships. It's a show that's both funny and heartfelt, with a great cast of characters.
8. **30 Rock**: A fast-paced and hilarious comedy that follows the behind-the-scenes antics of a fictional sketch comedy show. It's a show that's full of clever writing and great performances from Tina Fey and Alec Baldwin.

All of these shows have a similar tone and style to "Brooklyn Nine-Nine", with a focus on witty banter, silly humor, and heartwarming moments. I hope you find something that you enjoy!

content: User has been keeping up with 'The Good Place' on Hulu and loves it.
evidence_span: I've actually been keeping up with 'The Good Place' on Hulu, and I love it.
assistant_replies:
I'm so glad you're a fan of "The Good Place"!

If you're looking for shows similar to "Friends" or "How I Met Your Mother", I've got some great recommendations for you:

**Similar to "Friends"**

1. **New Girl**: As I mentioned earlier, it's a quirky and charming show about a group of friends living together in a Los Angeles loft. It has a similar feel to "Friends" with its witty banter, relatable characters, and hilarious situations.
2. **The Big Bang Theory**: While it's more focused on geek culture, it's a show about a group of friends navigating their lives, relationships, and careers. It has a similar "hangout" feel to "Friends".
3. **Happy Endings**: A short-lived but critically acclaimed show about a group of friends living in Chicago. It's fast-paced, witty, and has a similar tone to "Friends".
4. **Friends from College**: A Netflix original series that follows a group of friends from Harvard who are now in their 40s. It explores their relationships, careers, and personal growth.

**Similar to "How I Met Your Mother"**

1. **Happyish**: A comedy series that follows a group of friends navigating their 40s, relationships, and careers. It has a similar tone and style to "HIMYM".
2. **Don't Trust the B---- in Apartment 23**: A quirky show about a young woman who moves to New York City and becomes friends with her eccentric neighbor. It has a similar "buddy comedy" feel to "HIMYM".
3. **The League**: A comedy series about a group of friends who are part of a fantasy football league. It's raunchy, hilarious, and has a similar "guys' night out" feel to "HIMYM".
4. **Younger**: A comedy-drama series about a single mother in her 40s who pretends to be a 26-year-old in order to get a job in the competitive world of publishing. It has a similar "will-they-won't-they" romance feel to "HIMYM".

All of these shows have a similar tone, style, and focus on relationships and friendships that made "Friends" and "How I Met Your Mother" so beloved. I hope you find a new favorite show!
